description: Small, curated, offline seed corpus for the Signbridge Policy, Question, and Record agents. Text fields are policy summaries; quote fields are short source excerpts for UI citations.

chunks.title: Report damp and mould immediately
chunks.text: City of London Corporation Housing asks residents in City-managed properties to report damp and mould immediately. Reports should include where the mould is, how extensive it is, and whether anyone in the household is vulnerable. The page lists the Property Services reporting form, email route, and phone number.

chunks.title: Keep photos and reporting dates
chunks.locator: If you notice signs of damp or mould
chunks.text: The City damp and mould page tells residents to keep records when reporting damp or mould, including photos and the date the issue was reported.
chunks.quote: Take photos and note the date

chunks.title: City damp and mould response aims
chunks.section: Our approach to damp and mould
chunks.locator: Our approach to damp and mould
chunks.text: City of London Corporation says it aims to complete damp and mould inspections within 10 working days, standard mould treatments within 10 working days, severe mould treatment within 24 hours, standard leak investigation and repairs within 20 working days, and severe uncontainable leak emergency repairs within 24 hours.

chunks.title: Policy aim: dry, safe homes
chunks.text: The City damp, mould and condensation policy says its objectives include meeting landlord repairing responsibilities, maintaining homes to the Decent Homes Standard, and providing dry, safe homes free from damp and mould hazards.

chunks.title: No prejudging the cause
chunks.section: Aims of this Policy; Definitions and Causes
chunks.text: The City policy says residents reporting damp and mould should be treated with empathy and respect and that the cause should not be prejudged. The policy also says identifying condensation as a cause is not meant to imply blame on the resident.

chunks.title: Risk-based assessment includes vulnerability
chunks.text: The City policy says all reported damp and mould cases are assessed using a structured risk-based approach. The matrix considers severity, location, household composition, and whether individuals may be more vulnerable, such as young children, elderly residents, or people with health conditions.

chunks.title: City policy response aims by risk
chunks.text: The City damp and mould policy says high-risk cases or uncontrollable leaks aim for emergency repairs within 24 hours. Medium and low-risk cases are investigated within 10 working days, and essential repairs aim to commence within 5 working days of investigation.

chunks.title: Complex or serious health risk cases
chunks.text: The City policy says complex cases with significant remedial work may require temporary accommodation. Where there is a serious health risk to a resident or household member, emergency action could include emergency repair, mould treatment, or emergency temporary accommodation.

chunks.title: Groups at higher risk from damp and mould
chunks.text: The City policy says people at increased risk from damp and mould exposure include people with asthma or other lung conditions, children and young people, pregnant women, older people, people with weakened immune systems, people with mental health conditions, and people with mobility limitations.

chunks.title: Follow-up and repair records
chunks.section: Damp and Mould follow up; Evaluation of Repairs & Maintenance Data
chunks.text: For severe damp and mould cases, the City policy says it aims to revisit after six months to check the issue has not returned. The policy also says all damp and mould instances and actions taken in response will be recorded.
chunks.quote: revisit the property after six months

chunks.title: Accessible communication needs
chunks.text: The City policy says tenant needs should be considered so they are treated fairly. It says appropriate arrangements should ensure customers with distinct communication needs are not unreasonably and disproportionately affected, including interpretation or transcription where appropriate.

chunks.title: Complaint information to provide
chunks.section: Information you need to provide when making a complaint
chunks.text: The City housing complaints page says a formal complaint can be made if the issue has not been resolved. It asks for what the complaint is about, the date of the problem, what the Housing Team did wrong, what should be done to put things right, and any relevant documents.
chunks.quote: what should be done to put things right

chunks.title: Complaint definition and Ombudsman route
chunks.section: Definition of a complaint; The Housing Ombudsman
chunks.locator: Definition of a complaint; The Housing Ombudsman
chunks.text: The City page defines a housing complaint as dissatisfaction about service standard, action, or lack of action. Examples include failure to do something that should have been done, failure to meet service standards, or unfair treatment. The page says residents can contact the Housing Ombudsman Service at any stage of a complaint.

chunks.title: Awaab's Law summary for tenants
chunks.text: GOV.UK tenant guidance says social landlords must deal with emergency hazards and significant damp and mould hazards within set timescales. Emergency repairs must be done within 24 hours. Significant damp and mould hazards must be investigated within 10 working days and made safe within 5 working days after the investigation finishes.
chunks.quote: emergency repairs must be done within 24 hours

chunks.title: Written summary and further works
chunks.text: GOV.UK tenant guidance says the landlord must give a written summary of findings and how the home will be made safe within 3 working days after the investigation finishes. If more work is needed to stop the hazard returning, work must start or plans must be made within 5 working days, and all work must begin within 12 weeks at the latest.

chunks.title: Alternative accommodation if not safe in time
chunks.text: GOV.UK tenant guidance says if a landlord cannot make the home safe within Awaab's Law timescales, it must offer suitable alternative accommodation until the works needed to make the home safe for return are done.

chunks.title: Child asthma emergency damp and mould example
chunks.section: Emergency damp and mould hazard examples
chunks.locator: Emergency damp and mould hazard examples
chunks.text: GOV.UK landlord guidance gives an example where damp is prevalent in a child's bedroom and the tenant reports that the child's asthma has been affected. In that example, the landlord assesses it as a potential emergency hazard and arranges attendance within 24 hours.

chunks.title: Regulatory context: repairs and maintenance
chunks.text: The February 2026 regulatory judgement says the City of London Corporation had recognised a need to improve repairs and maintenance timeliness and effectiveness. It concludes there were serious failings in delivering required Safety and Quality Standard outcomes relating to repairs, maintenance, and planned improvements.

chunks.title: Tenant satisfaction and repair performance context
chunks.text: The City tenant satisfaction measures page reports 2024-2025 figures including satisfaction with repairs, homes not meeting the Decent Homes Standard, and emergency and non-emergency repairs completed within target timescales. These are performance context, not individual entitlement rules.

chunks.title: London open data foundation
chunks.text: The London Datastore is the Greater London Authority open data-sharing portal for London and indexes datasets across themes including housing, health, communities, and London as a world city.

chunks.title: Housing in London dataset context
chunks.section: Housing in London 2025 report and tables
chunks.locator: Housing in London 2025 report and tables
chunks.text: The London Datastore Housing in London dataset page provides the 2025 report and spreadsheet tables used as the evidence base for the Mayor's housing strategy. It is suitable for London-wide housing context and deployment targeting, not individual policy cards.

chunks.title: ONS Census 2021 disability context
chunks.text: The ONS disability dataset provides Census 2021 estimates for usual residents in England and Wales by long-term health problem or disability, sex, age, and deprivation as of Census Day, 21 March 2021. It is context only until an exact hearing or BSL borough table is confirmed.
